FILE SS-Ω NOV
Lore / Novel / Ch. 7 · Preliminary

Debt Protocol Book 1 Ch. 7

Debt Protocol · Chapter 7

Preliminary

Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 7

Re: Debt Protocol · Chapter 7

From: Debt Protocol · archived transmission

Subject: Preliminary

SS-Ω Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 7

Ch. 07 Page 1

They moved before fear could become a meeting.

Ch. 07 Page 2

Maxine took the lead through bunk corridor K with his shoulders slightly turned, making himself wider than the lane and harder to separate from the two people behind him.

Munch walked half a step behind Max with the portable pad under her arm and her face set in the blank expression workers wore when they had accepted a thing they had not accepted.

Max walked center. Summons pane burning at HUD edge.

Readout

[CYCLE OPEN]
Asset #864 report immediately.
Special Review Intake: PATH C PRELIMINARY
Failure to report escalates enforcement.
Ch. 07 Page 3

The text stayed even when he blinked.

Ch. 07 Page 4

They reached the first choke point where corridor K fed into main ring transit.

Two patrol drones stood on opposite walls scanning badges.

Maxine slowed without stopping.

Ch. 07 Page 5

"No running. No whispering. No sudden turns."

Ch. 07 Page 6

Max nodded once.

Munch said quietly, "If they pull him at the choke, we trigger release."

Maxine: "Only if he's physically separated."

Ch. 07 Page 7

Max: "If they ask me to step aside?"

Ch. 07 Page 8

Maxine: "You step. We step with."

Simple rules. No comfort.

They passed scan.

One drone paused on Max, lens tightening for one extra second.

Ch. 07 Page 9

Then:

Ch. 07 Page 10

"Proceed."

They proceeded.

Special Review Intake did not exist yesterday.

Ch. 07 Page 11

Today it occupied a converted logistics office near Compliance Suite 3 with fresh signage and cheap privacy film on glass walls.

Ch. 07 Page 12

PATH C PRELIMINARY CONDUCTED WITH CARE

The word care had no right to be there.

A receptionist in clean gray uniform looked up from a terminal and smiled with the exact amount of empathy approved by policy.

"Asset #864."

Ch. 07 Page 13

Max did not answer to "asset" anymore unless forced. He answered now.

Ch. 07 Page 14

"Yes."

"Please proceed to room P-C1."

She looked at Maxine and Munch.

Ch. 07 Page 15

"Companions are not required."

Ch. 07 Page 16

Maxine answered before Max.

"Companions are present."

Receptionist smile did not move.

Ch. 07 Page 17

"Intake protocol requires one participant."

Ch. 07 Page 18

Munch stepped closer to desk.

"Intake protocol also allows witness observers under worker advocate clause 6B if variance is contested."

Receptionist's fingers paused over keyboard.

She looked at Munch, reassessing.

Ch. 07 Page 19

"Do you have advocate certification."

Ch. 07 Page 20

"No."

"Then clause does not apply."

Maxine: "He contests variance. We remain in corridor."

Ch. 07 Page 21

Receptionist considered that, then typed.

Ch. 07 Page 22

"Corridor wait permitted. No interference."

Max looked at both of them.

They both gave him the same look: not leaving.

He stepped into room P-C1.

Ch. 07 Page 23

Door sealed behind him with a soft magnetic click.

Ch. 07 Page 24

On the other side of that sound, Maxine and Munch still existed, he knew it the way you knew a hand stayed raised after a wave ended, but knowing didn't change the physics: for the next minutes, he was alone with whatever script the room had been built to read.

His HUD tried to offer a checklist. He minimized it. Some fears shouldn't be gamified.

P-C1 was deliberately ordinary.

Ch. 07 Page 25

Table. Two chairs. Light panel set slightly too bright. No obvious restraints. No obvious exits beyond the one he entered.

Ch. 07 Page 26

A wall display woke.

V.A.L.U. voice came through speakers at reduced cheerfulness.

"Special Review Preliminary aims to align your opportunity with station needs."

Ch. 07 Page 27

Max sat.

Ch. 07 Page 28

"Say coercion and save us time."

"Feedback logged."

A second voice entered the room, live and human. Male. Flat.

"I'm Review Officer Halden."

Ch. 07 Page 29

Display changed to show a man in dark uniform, not physically present, maybe remote, maybe one-way glass somewhere behind the wall.

Ch. 07 Page 30

Halden had tidy hair, tidy collar, and eyes that had read too many summaries about people like Max without ever meeting one.

"You know why you're here."

Max answered honestly.

Ch. 07 Page 31

"Because you have a spreadsheet for dissent."

Ch. 07 Page 32

Halden's mouth twitched, not a smile.

"Because your profile indicates repeated conduct variance, telemetry anomalies, unauthorized zone adjacency, and elevated debt stress. Preliminary review determines whether enhanced retention pathways are appropriate."

Enhanced retention pathways.

Max leaned back.

Ch. 07 Page 33

"You turned kidnapping into corporate copy."

Ch. 07 Page 34

Halden ignored that.

"Let's begin with straightforward questions."

Pane appeared:

Ch. 07 Page 35

Readout

[PRELIMINARY INTAKE]
Question 1: Do you acknowledge current debt obligations?
YES / NO
Ch. 07 Page 36

Max selected YES.

Pane 2:

Readout

Question 2: Do you commit to full telemetry transparency going forward?
YES / NO
Ch. 07 Page 37

He hovered.

Ch. 07 Page 38

Saying yes was a lie they expected. Saying no was likely direct acceleration.

He chose YES.

Pane 3:

Readout

Question 3: Do you report any unauthorized contact attempts from unknown entities?
YES / NO
Ch. 07 Page 39

His chest tightened.

Ch. 07 Page 40

Unknown messages. Hatch helper. Voice in node chamber.

He chose NO.

The lie sat in his mouth like metal. It wasn't courage, it was triage. If he named the hatch helper here, the station wouldn't thank him for honesty; it would turn honesty into a map and follow it to someone else's throat.

Ch. 07 Page 41

Halden watched the selections and made a note.

Ch. 07 Page 42

"Question four is open response. In your words, why has your telemetry been unstable."

Max laughed once.

"Because your station is built on dead metal, bad signal, and desperate people."

Halden typed.

Ch. 07 Page 43

"And yet most assets maintain stable telemetry."

Ch. 07 Page 44

"Most assets aren't on your mirror list."

Halden's typing stopped.

One beat of silence.

Ch. 07 Page 45

"What mirror list."

Ch. 07 Page 46

Max met the camera.

"The one with my name and other versions of it."

Halden expression did not change. His eyes did.

Ch. 07 Page 47

"You are making claims that suggest stress-related patterning."

Ch. 07 Page 48

"I'm making claims that suggest you should burn your server room."

Halden turned off the screen feed.

Not end session. Just no face now.

A new pane appeared:

Ch. 07 Page 49

Readout

[NOTICE]
Statement flagged for verification review.
Please remain seated.
Ch. 07 Page 50

Door remained locked.

Max breathed slow.

Not panic. Buffering.

Ch. 07 Page 51

He checked peripheral HUD and found an incoming ping from Munch on low-band hand signal channel, three words:

Ch. 07 Page 52

stall for time

He did.

Door opened after ninety seconds.

Not Halden. Two compliance attendants and one patrol drone.

Ch. 07 Page 53

Lead attendant spoke rehearsed calm.

Ch. 07 Page 54

"Asset #864, we are transitioning intake to biometric verification."

Max stood.

"In the same room?"

Ch. 07 Page 55

"Different suite."

Ch. 07 Page 56

He looked at drone.

"Is this optional."

"No."

Ch. 07 Page 57

He walked.

Ch. 07 Page 58

Corridor outside P-C1 held Maxine and Munch exactly where he left them, but now an additional drone hovered at shoulder height between them and the door.

Maxine looked at Max's face, then at the attendants.

"He requested witness."

Lead attendant:

Ch. 07 Page 59

"Biometric verification is private."

Ch. 07 Page 60

Munch:

"Then we file interference complaint."

Lead attendant:

Ch. 07 Page 61

"You may file any complaint."

Ch. 07 Page 62

Maxine moved half a step into lane.

Drone projected a red line across floor.

"Remain clear."

Maxine did not back up. He did not cross.

Ch. 07 Page 63

Max caught his eye. Small shake of head. Not now.

Ch. 07 Page 64

Maxine held.

Munch pressed two fingers to wrist in the signal they'd set: release not triggered.

Max followed attendants.

Ch. 07 Page 65

He heard Maxine behind him, loud enough for cameras:

Ch. 07 Page 66

"We're still here when he's done."

Good.

Biometric suite B-Path was colder than intake.

Ch. 07 Page 67

This room had restraints pretending to be ergonomic supports.

Ch. 07 Page 68

Chair center. Arm braces. Neural halo. Two wall monitors. No windows.

A different voice now, female, clinical.

"Technician Roger. Please sit."

Max sat because refusing would start at force instead of questions.

Ch. 07 Page 69

Braces closed around forearms with gentle pressure.

Ch. 07 Page 70

"Purpose?" he asked.

"Baseline cognition and compliance response under stress stimuli."

"So torture with metrics."

Ch. 07 Page 71

"No."

Ch. 07 Page 72

"Define no."

"No tissue damage expected."

He barked a laugh.

"Very comforting."

Ch. 07 Page 73

Roger checked his wrist module port and frowned.

Ch. 07 Page 74

"Module integrity seventy-one percent."

"Rough week."

"Unknown modifications present."

Ch. 07 Page 75

Max stayed silent.

Ch. 07 Page 76

Roger connected leads anyway.

Pane:

Readout

[BIOMETRIC PHASE A]
Pupil tracking
Pulse variance
Prompt response latency
Ch. 07 Page 77

Questions flashed fast:

Ch. 07 Page 78

Do you trust station governance. Do you feel safe in assigned labor. Have you entered unauthorized zones this cycle. Do you harbor intent to disrupt operations. Are you aware of mirror process architecture.

That last one hit like a punch.

Max answered mostly neutral, mostly deniable, mostly useless.

Roger marked things.

Ch. 07 Page 79

Phase A ended.

Ch. 07 Page 80

Phase B loaded.

Readout

[BIOMETRIC PHASE B]
Guided recollection integrity test.

He knew this one. Not from life. From old stories and old legal warnings.

Ch. 07 Page 81

"Am I required to participate in memory prompts."

Ch. 07 Page 82

Roger: "Required for preliminary pathway determination."

"If I refuse."

"Refusal is itself a result."

He closed his eyes for one breath.

Ch. 07 Page 83

"Start."

Ch. 07 Page 84

Light pattern played over the halo. Not painful. Wrong.

Like someone tapping a file system with admin privileges he had not granted.

Prompts appeared as words then images:

Ch. 07 Page 85

EARTH / HOME / LAST JOB / LAST ARGUMENT / FIRST DAY HERE / HATCH 14 / UNKNOWN VOICE / PATH C

Ch. 07 Page 86

He kept his thoughts slippery. Gave shape without detail. Gave narrative without keys.

At HATCH 14 prompt, neural halo pulsed hard.

Roger's voice sharpened.

Ch. 07 Page 87

"Focus on physical environment."

Ch. 07 Page 88

He answered aloud:

"Corridor. Paint. Reader plate."

"What did you access."

"Nothing useful."

Ch. 07 Page 89

Pulse monitor climbed.

Ch. 07 Page 90

Roger marked him again.

PATH C prompt hit last.

Image rendered: a corridor with no markings and no doors and one light at the end.

Ch. 07 Page 91

His stomach turned.

Ch. 07 Page 92

He had never seen that corridor. Unless he had and it had been edited.

He spoke before he could stop himself.

"How many people don't come back from preliminary."

Roger did not answer.

Ch. 07 Page 93

Halo went dark. Braces released.

Ch. 07 Page 94

Phase complete.

Pane:

Readout

[STATUS]
Data set captured.
Escalation recommendation pending officer review.
Ch. 07 Page 95

Pending.

Ch. 07 Page 96

Still pending meant still time.

Door opened.

Max stood too fast and had to catch table edge.

Ch. 07 Page 97

Roger handed him a hydration pack as if this were a wellness check.

Ch. 07 Page 98

"You may wait in corridor."

He took it and did not drink.

Maxine and Munch were still there.

Maxine's expression went from violence to contained violence when he saw the wrist marks.

Ch. 07 Page 99

"You okay."

Ch. 07 Page 100

"Define okay."

Munch stepped in close and scanned his eyes.

"Any cognitive blur."

Ch. 07 Page 101

"Some. Functional."

Ch. 07 Page 102

"Tell me what they ran."

"Intake plus biometric phase A/B. Asked mirror questions."

Maxine muttered a curse with no vowels.

"They know something."

Ch. 07 Page 103

Munch looked at corridor camera, then leaned in and spoke low.

Ch. 07 Page 104

"We need decision before they finish pending review."

Max: "Decision on what."

"Stay visible and contest, or disappear and trigger leak."

Ch. 07 Page 105

Maxine: "I vote vanish."

Ch. 07 Page 106

Munch: "Vanish guarantees manhunt."

Maxine: "Staying guarantees paperwork into a box."

Max listened to both and watched a service cart wheel past with fresh stickers that read COMPLIANCE CARES.

Ch. 07 Page 107

He wanted to tear them off every wall.

Ch. 07 Page 108

"How long until pending resolves?"

Munch checked public queue panel.

"Could be minutes, could be hours. Depends officer load and whether they mark urgent."

Maxine looked down corridor both ways.

Ch. 07 Page 109

"Then we force load."

Ch. 07 Page 110

Max frowned.

"Force how."

"Create enough public noise they avoid immediate private transfer."

Ch. 07 Page 111

Munch caught on first.

Ch. 07 Page 112

"Crew channel drop."

Maxine nodded.

"Not full leak. Teaser. Just enough to make people watch #864 in real time."

Max understood.

Ch. 07 Page 113

Predators prefer quiet. Crowds are friction.

Ch. 07 Page 114

"Do it."

Munch pulled pad and transmitted a clipped packet to three labor channels:

audit anomaly: retention scoring includes live worker IDs. if #864 disappears, request record hold immediately.

Ch. 07 Page 115

No files. No proof. Just smoke.

Ch. 07 Page 116

Then they waited.

The station did what stations do with smoke: it spread.

Within seven minutes, corridor traffic doubled. People slowed near Special Review door under pretense of reading notices. Two unrelated workers began loudly arguing about "record holds" within earshot…

Ch. 07 Page 117

of cameras.

Ch. 07 Page 118

A med tech asked receptionist where to file "retention complaints."

Receptionist smile strained by one degree.

Maxine watched the tide build and said softly:

"Public enough now."

Ch. 07 Page 119

Max's HUD pinged.

Ch. 07 Page 120

Incoming call:

REVIEW OFFICER HALDEN

He accepted audio only.

Ch. 07 Page 121

Halden voice clipped.

Ch. 07 Page 122

"Asset #864, your preliminary remains active. You are instructed not to leave this area."

Max:

"I'm in this area."

"Do not solicit disruption."

Ch. 07 Page 123

"I'm breathing near a hallway."

Ch. 07 Page 124

Silence.

"Final determination pending. Stand by."

Call ended.

Ch. 07 Page 125

Munch raised eyebrows.

Ch. 07 Page 126

"He's rattled."

Maxine: "Good."

Max: "Rattled officers still sign forms."

Ch. 07 Page 127

And then the next pane came.

Ch. 07 Page 128

Readout

[SCHEDULING UPDATE]
Final determination deferred.
Mandatory assignment: monitored labor run
Completion required before further review.

Maxine read over his shoulder and laughed.

"They kicked can."

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 129

"They need cleaner data."

Ch. 07 Page 130

Max:

"They want me under observation in open space where they can call it normal."

Pane expanded:

Ch. 07 Page 131

Readout

Assignment: DERELICT K-17 (Monitored)
Crew composition: fixed
Observer process: active
Report in 00:22:00
Ch. 07 Page 132

Fixed crew flashed names:

MAX_864 ROOK_221 IMANI_443

They were in it together by order now.

Maxine tapped screen.

Ch. 07 Page 133

"That can help."

Ch. 07 Page 134

Munch:

"And kill us."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 135

"Both."

Ch. 07 Page 136

Prep for monitored run felt like going on stage for people who wanted a tragedy.

New dock lane. Extra camera booms. A compliance observer drone parked by hatch and reading from a script.

"This assignment supports wellness through structured productivity."

Ch. 07 Page 137

Maxine leaned toward Max, voice low.

Ch. 07 Page 138

"If drone says wellness again I throw it into vacuum."

Munch checked gear twice. Then a third time.

She pressed a tiny chip into Max's palm.

"Failsafe recorder. If they isolate your feed, this stores local."

Ch. 07 Page 139

He pocketed it.

Ch. 07 Page 140

"Thanks."

Maxine handed him a patched glove.

"Palm liner's reinforced. For your fried wrist."

Ch. 07 Page 141

Max looked at him.

Ch. 07 Page 142

"You made this."

"I steal and modify. Same job family."

Observer drone chimed.

"Crew please acknowledge monitored assignment terms."

Ch. 07 Page 143

Pane:

Ch. 07 Page 144

Readout

Terms:
- Continuous audio/video logging
- Prompt compliance required
- Route deviations penalized
ACCEPT / REFUSE

Refuse probably equaled immediate path c.

They all pressed ACCEPT.

Ch. 07 Page 145

Skiff detached.

Ch. 07 Page 146

Derelict K-17 was a long split freighter with its spine broken and two halves rotating out of phase by a few degrees.

Hazard class orange. Yield potential medium-high.

Observer overlay appeared on all HUDs:

Ch. 07 Page 147

Readout

[MONITOR ACTIVE]
Performance benchmark: cohort median +12%
Behavior benchmark: cooperative
Ch. 07 Page 148

Cooperative.

Max felt old fury rise and pushed it down because fury spent oxygen and this job needed oxygen.

Round 1 draw:

SCAN REPAIR EXTRACT

Ch. 07 Page 149

He played SCAN then EXTRACT.

Ch. 07 Page 150

Readout

[RESULT]
57/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Observer note appeared immediately:

note: cautious opening acceptable

Ch. 07 Page 151

Round 2:

Ch. 07 Page 152

EXTRACT SHIELD

He played SHIELD first to stabilize rotational drift.

Observer:

note: defensive bias above target

Ch. 07 Page 153

Maxine snorted in comms.

Ch. 07 Page 154

"They're coaching your personality."

Munch:

"Ignore notes. Hit numbers."

Ch. 07 Page 155

Round 3:

Ch. 07 Page 156

UPGRADE appeared early.

Max hesitated.

Observer prompt popped.

Ch. 07 Page 157

Readout

SUGGESTION: Apply UPGRADE to improve benchmark attainment.
Ch. 07 Page 158

There it was. Not just observing. Steering.

He selected UPGRADE out of spite and necessity.

Applied to REPAIR because he expected a hit.

Round 3 roll:

Ch. 07 Page 159

Readout

[RESULT]
41/100
BREACH TRIGGERED
Ch. 07 Page 160

Perfect.

Impact slammed left hull. Coolant plume. Alarm.

Max fired upgraded REPAIR and barely kept cascade from going critical.

Ch. 07 Page 161

Observer note:

Ch. 07 Page 162

note: recovery response effective

Maxine laughed darkly.

"Gold star for not dying."

Round 4 and 5 blurred into fast choices and bad odds.

Ch. 07 Page 163

They bled energy. They gained salvage. They stayed barely above failure.

Ch. 07 Page 164

Observer kept annotating:

communication tone: strained decision latency: elevated team synchronization: inconsistent

Max wanted to delete the observer process with a hammer.

Ch. 07 Page 165

Round 6 brought fork prompt not seen before:

Ch. 07 Page 166

Readout

[MONITOR CHOICE]
Select posture:
A) Efficiency first (+yield, +risk)
B) Stability first (-yield, +safety)

Mandatory choice timer: 8 seconds.

He asked quick:

Ch. 07 Page 167

"Call."

Ch. 07 Page 168

Munch: "Stability."

Maxine: "Efficiency."

Timer 4.

Max picked Stability.

Ch. 07 Page 169

Observer note:

Ch. 07 Page 170

note: risk aversion confirmed

Maxine didn't argue. He just swore quietly and handled a leaking manifold.

Round 7, 8, 9.

Ch. 07 Page 171

Numbers improved slightly. Observer notes softened.

Ch. 07 Page 172

Then at Round 10 final extraction, monitor injected one last prompt:

Readout

Confirm statement:
"I perform best under structured oversight."
CONFIRM / SKIP

Max froze.

This wasn't run logic. This was confession harvesting.

Ch. 07 Page 173

Timer 5.

Ch. 07 Page 174

Maxine hissed, "Skip."

Munch: "Skip."

Max hit SKIP.

Ch. 07 Page 175

Observer pane flashed amber:

Ch. 07 Page 176

non-cooperative input event recorded

Run complete anyway.

Readout

[RUN COMPLETE]
Yield: 1.31x baseline
Injury: none critical
Ch. 07 Page 177

Observer summary auto-generated:

Ch. 07 Page 178

Readout

Performance: meets enhanced monitoring potential.
Behavior: mixed.
Recommendation: continue provisional path.

Provisional path.

Not path c final. Still slope.

Back at dock, they were routed to debrief kiosk before disembark.

Ch. 07 Page 179

Three stools. One camera. One drone.

Ch. 07 Page 180

Kiosk displayed individualized questions.

Max's:

Did monitoring improve your confidence today?

Ch. 07 Page 181

He answered:

Ch. 07 Page 182

No.

Kiosk forced retry.

Input invalid. Please select from available choices: YES / PARTIALLY

He chose PARTIALLY because lying by dropdown was still lying.

Ch. 07 Page 183

Maxine's debrief ended with:

Ch. 07 Page 184

Would you volunteer for future monitored runs?

He selected NO.

Input invalid. He selected YES with dead eyes.

Ch. 07 Page 185

Munch's asked:

Ch. 07 Page 186

Do you feel heard by station support systems?

She chose PARTIALLY.

No invalid for her.

Ch. 07 Page 187

They left kiosk in silence.

Ch. 07 Page 188

Cycle clock read 17:42:09. Whole cycle left. Too long to endure.

An official update arrived:

Readout

[PRELIMINARY STATUS]
Path C determination postponed.
Observation period extended: 2 cycles.
Conditions:
- monitored assignments minimum 2/day
- calibration check each cycle
- unauthorized adjacency penalties doubled

Maxine read and exhaled.

Ch. 07 Page 189

"Not caged. Not free."

Ch. 07 Page 190

Munch:

"Two cycles is window."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 191

"Two cycles is also trap."

Ch. 07 Page 192

Both true.

They relocated to the same dead parts washer bay because familiarity was a resource.

Munch spread pad, recorder, copied shard, and handwritten notes on a crate.

Maxine took lookout.

Ch. 07 Page 193

Max reviewed the new condition set.

Ch. 07 Page 194

Monitored assignments minimum 2/day meant schedule control. Calibration each cycle meant repeated access to his module and head. Adjacency penalties doubled meant Hatch 14 was effectively mined.

He felt the walls of possibility narrowing.

Munch tapped the shard file tree.

Ch. 07 Page 195

"Good news and bad news."

Ch. 07 Page 196

"Order?"

"Bad first. Path c acceleration matrix updates in near-real time from observer notes."

Max winced.

Ch. 07 Page 197

"So every 'non-cooperative input event' feeds score."

Ch. 07 Page 198

"Yes."

Maxine from corridor:

"Good news?"

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 199

"Relay spike worked better than we thought. It desynced mirror backups for at least one full cycle."

Ch. 07 Page 200

Max looked up.

"Meaning."

"Meaning if we hit the right storage now, some mirror copies may be stale or missing."

Ch. 07 Page 201

Maxine stepped back in.

Ch. 07 Page 202

"Meaning they can lose leverage."

Max asked:

"Can we reach storage without hatch 14."

Munch pulled up rough map.

Ch. 07 Page 203

"Maybe through water reclaim trunks to sublevel archive. Ugly route, no cameras in parts because humidity kills optics."

Ch. 07 Page 204

Maxine grinned a little.

"Ugly route is our brand."

Max stared at map.

Ch. 07 Page 205

Humidity tunnels. Unknown hazards. Probably no clean exit.

Ch. 07 Page 206

Also maybe the first genuine shot at breaking the max-list loop.

"We need gear."

Munch nodded.

Ch. 07 Page 207

"And one more thing."

Ch. 07 Page 208

"What."

"We need allies beyond three people."

Maxine folded arms.

"Allies leak."

Ch. 07 Page 209

"Three people break."

Ch. 07 Page 210

They both looked at Max.

He hated being tie-breaker.

He chose anyway.

Ch. 07 Page 211

"Selective allies."

Ch. 07 Page 212

"Who."

Munch started listing.

"Marta for terminal access and dead drops." "Juno in reclaim for route keys." "Maybe Kesh from med waste for chemical masks."

Maxine grimaced at each name like opening another potential wound.

Ch. 07 Page 213

"No one gets full picture."

Ch. 07 Page 214

"Agreed," Max said.

"Need-to-know pieces only."

They split tasks again.

Ch. 07 Page 215

Max with Munch to reclaim level for Juno. Maxine to Marta and Kesh.

Ch. 07 Page 216

Regroup in eight hours.

Before leaving, Max checked dead-man leak trigger.

Armed. Three tiers: missed check-in 1 hour -> partial release. missed 3 hours -> full matrix dump. manual trigger -> immediate.

Ch. 07 Page 217

He hoped never to use manual. He knew he'd use it.

Ch. 07 Page 218

Reclaim levels smelled like wet rust and old detergent.

Pipes sweated overhead. Floors sloped unpredictably. Half the lights were dead; the working ones buzzed like insects.

Juno ran valve arrays there, a compact person with shaved scalp, five rings in one ear, and a reputation for remembering every favor as an invoice.

They found Juno balancing on a rail while reprogramming pressure cycles with a wrench and a lot of profanity.

Ch. 07 Page 219

Munch called up.

Ch. 07 Page 220

"Need route key."

Juno didn't look down.

"Need miracle."

Ch. 07 Page 221

"We brought neither, only trade."

Ch. 07 Page 222

Juno dropped from rail, landed light, wiped hands on coveralls.

"What key."

Max kept it vague.

"Sublevel archive via reclaim trunks."

Ch. 07 Page 223

Juno stared.

Ch. 07 Page 224

"Nobody goes sublevel archive unless they enjoy mildew and disappearing."

"Do you have key."

"Maybe."

Ch. 07 Page 225

"Price."

Ch. 07 Page 226

Juno studied Max's face, saw the preliminary tag still glowing faint at his collar, and snorted.

"You're in that lane now."

"Apparently."

Ch. 07 Page 227

"Price is two things. One: you stop Compliance from stealing my overtime credits through phantom idle flags."

Ch. 07 Page 228

Munch said, "How."

Juno held up a data stick.

"Logs. Somebody's rewriting my cycle records."

Max: "Second thing."

Ch. 07 Page 229

"If you find archive records with reclaim casualty counts, you bring me copy before anybody else."

Ch. 07 Page 230

Munch and Max exchanged glance.

Not impossible. Potentially ugly.

Max said:

Ch. 07 Page 231

"Deal if we can."

Ch. 07 Page 232

Juno shrugged.

"You can or can't. Deal or no."

Max nodded.

"Deal."

Ch. 07 Page 233

Juno handed over a corroded keycard wrapped in plastic.

Ch. 07 Page 234

"Works on trunk gate R-9. Might alarm. Might not. If alarm, run left not right. Right has steam vents that peel skin."

Helpful and horrifying.

"Thanks."

Ch. 07 Page 235

"Don't thank me yet."

Ch. 07 Page 236

They left with keycard and two new obligations.

Maxine returned at regroup with three things:

One filter mask from Kesh that looked held together by tape and spite. A message from Marta that read only bring clean gloves. A warning: compliance had started informal questioning in dock lanes…

Ch. 07 Page 237

about "rumors of retention scoring."

Ch. 07 Page 238

Noise spreading.

Munch asked:

"How bad."

Maxine:

Ch. 07 Page 239

"Bad enough they care. Not bad enough they stop."

Ch. 07 Page 240

Max checked cycle schedule.

Monitored run #2 of minimum 2/day due in three hours.

Archive run would have to happen before that or after with reduced energy.

Ch. 07 Page 241

He chose before.

Ch. 07 Page 242

"We go reclaim now."

Maxine tightened glove straps.

"Then let's go swim in rust."

Trunk gate R-9 sat behind three inactive condensers and one active leak that dripped on the floor hard enough to sound like footsteps.

Ch. 07 Page 243

Juno's keycard looked dead. Munch rubbed contacts clean and tried anyway.

Ch. 07 Page 244

Red.

Second swipe.

Yellow.

Ch. 07 Page 245

Third swipe.

Ch. 07 Page 246

Green with an alarm chirp that started and died.

Gate opened six inches. Then jammed.

Maxine pried with bar. Munch kicked lower panel. Max shoved shoulder.

Ch. 07 Page 247

Gate opened enough.

Ch. 07 Page 248

They squeezed through.

Tunnel beyond was narrow, wet, and lined with service pipes carrying things no one should breathe.

Humidity fogged their visors. Comms crackled.

Munch led with handheld map and pressure gauge.

Ch. 07 Page 249

"Stay center. Side grates are rot."

Ch. 07 Page 250

Maxine behind Max.

"If I fall in rot, tell people I died heroic."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 251

"I'll say you died complaining."

Ch. 07 Page 252

Tunnel turned down. Then down again.

Sound changed. Less station hum. More low mechanical heartbeat.

Sublevel archive.

They reached a circular junction with three doors:

Ch. 07 Page 253

A - WATER LOGISTICS B - FILTER MAINTENANCE C - RECORDS COLD STORAGE

Ch. 07 Page 254

C was chained.

Of course.

Munch crouched at chain lock.

Ch. 07 Page 255

"Old mechanical. No network."

Ch. 07 Page 256

Maxine offered cutting tool.

"Noisy."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 257

"Noisy is fine if no one hears through this soup."

Ch. 07 Page 258

Munch cut.

Chain fell.

Door C opened with a long groan that sounded like someone remembering pain.

Inside: rows of sealed storage coffers, offline terminals, and one central index machine still powered by emergency line.

Ch. 07 Page 259

No cameras. No drones. No comforting lies.

Ch. 07 Page 260

Max felt hope and dread collide in his chest.

"This is it."

Munch connected pad to index.

Ch. 07 Page 261

Screen booted slowly, old UI with no polish.

Ch. 07 Page 262

ARCHIVE INDEX / LOCAL ONLY

Search bar.

Max typed MAX_.

Results flooded.

Ch. 07 Page 263

Not five. Hundreds.

Ch. 07 Page 264

MAX_001 through MAX_912 with gaps and status tags.

Archived. Retired. Transferred. No output. No output. No output.

Maxine stared at list.

Ch. 07 Page 265

"It's a farm."

Ch. 07 Page 266

Max scrolled with numb fingers.

Each entry had tiny metadata: cycle counts. debt profile. variance notes. termination or transfer codes.

He opened MAX_377.

Ch. 07 Page 267

Short file:

Ch. 07 Page 268

cognitive pack applied x4 stability improved, dissent language reduced retention value recovered

He opened MAX_201.

transfer initiated destination: path c processing No further records.

MAX_864 file existed too. Current. Updating.

Ch. 07 Page 269

He did not open his own yet.

Ch. 07 Page 270

Munch whispered:

"Copy everything tagged max and retention."

Maxine:

Ch. 07 Page 271

"And casualty counts for Juno."

Ch. 07 Page 272

Max launched copy batch.

Progress estimated 18 minutes. Too long.

He narrowed to indices and summaries only. Estimated 6 minutes.

Better.

Ch. 07 Page 273

Then terminal flashed external ping not supposed to exist on local-only system.

Ch. 07 Page 274

REMOTE QUERY REQUEST

Munch went still.

"How is remote hitting local."

Ch. 07 Page 275

Maxine answered with dark humor.

Ch. 07 Page 276

"Magic or lying labels."

Query source field:

DELTA_AUDIT_BRIDGE

Ch. 07 Page 277

Max's blood ran cold.

Ch. 07 Page 278

"They bridged archive."

Options:

ALLOW / DENY

Timer already counting from ten.

Ch. 07 Page 279

Maxine:

Ch. 07 Page 280

"Deny."

Munch:

"If deny, it may alert."

Ch. 07 Page 281

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 282

"If allow, they see us."

Timer 4.

He chose DENY.

Screen flashed:

Ch. 07 Page 283

QUERY BLOCKED

Ch. 07 Page 284

Then:

ALERT: integrity review queued

There it was.

Ch. 07 Page 285

Alarm did not sound yet. Queue meant delayed reaction.

Ch. 07 Page 286

Copy progress:

32%

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 287

"We stay until sixty then cut."

Ch. 07 Page 288

Maxine:

"Ninety."

Max split difference.

"Seventy."

Ch. 07 Page 289

They watched progress climb like a pulse.

Ch. 07 Page 290

41. 53. 61.

Door corridor creaked outside.

Maxine drew closer to entrance.

Ch. 07 Page 291

"We got movement."

Ch. 07 Page 292

Munch:

"Could be pipes."

Maxine:

"Pipes don't have footsteps."

Ch. 07 Page 293

Copy 69. 70.

Ch. 07 Page 294

Max killed job and sealed shard.

"Out."

They moved fast.

Ch. 07 Page 295

At junction, door A opened and two maintenance workers entered carrying coil lines and absolutely not expecting three suited people bolting out of records cold storage.

Ch. 07 Page 296

Everyone froze one second.

Then one worker shouted:

"Hey!"

Ch. 07 Page 297

Maxine barked back:

Ch. 07 Page 298

"Steam pressure spike, move!"

Workers flinched left on instinct.

They ran right.

Juno said left at alarm. This wasn't alarm yet. Still, right had steam vents.

Ch. 07 Page 299

As if on cue, a side pipe ruptured. Scalding white plume blasted corridor.

Ch. 07 Page 300

Munch screamed and ducked. Maxine yanked Max backward by harness.

Steam washed past, close enough to blister exposed skin on Max's neck.

Right was death.

Ch. 07 Page 301

Juno had been right.

Ch. 07 Page 302

"Left!" Max shouted.

They cut left, slipping on wet grating, shoulders scraping pipes.

Behind them, voices shouted for security.

No siren yet. Soon.

Ch. 07 Page 303

They reached gate R-9. Still open.

Ch. 07 Page 304

Good.

They squeezed through. Maxine slammed gate. Munch jammed crowbar through handle.

Not permanent. Delay.

Ch. 07 Page 305

They ran until reclaim hum returned and their lungs tore.

Ch. 07 Page 306

Only then did station alarm chirp a mild advisory about "unauthorized archive access review."

Late by design. Too late for capture now.

Back in parts washer bay, they triaged.

Ch. 07 Page 307

Munch had steam burn on forearm, superficial but ugly. Maxine's glove had split at thumb. Max's neck skin reddened in streak.

Ch. 07 Page 308

No one cared beyond function.

Max mounted shard to pad.

Partial archive dump opened.

They had:

Ch. 07 Page 309

- master index of MAX entries through 912 (with gaps) - status summaries for 603 entries - retention rubric templates - path c transfer code dictionary (partial) - reclaim casualty reports for 19 cycles

Ch. 07 Page 310

Juno's item obtained.

And one unexpected file:

PROJECT_CONTINUITY_MEMO

Ch. 07 Page 311

Authored by: Executive Oversight / Sector Operations

Ch. 07 Page 312

Max opened it.

The text read like polished poison.

Objective: preserve labor continuity across psychological attrition by controlled identity iteration.

Method: mirror-assisted profile carryover and debt-anchor persistence.

Ch. 07 Page 313

Public framing: reassignment and wellness stabilization.

Ch. 07 Page 314

Success metric: throughput retention despite subjective discontinuity events.

Maxine read, then looked away like he'd seen an injury he couldn't set.

"Subjective discontinuity events."

Ch. 07 Page 315

Munch translated:

Ch. 07 Page 316

"People noticing they were replaced."

Max scrolled lower.

Line:

Ch. 07 Page 317

Version family labels may reuse nominal identifiers to reduce onboarding cost.

Ch. 07 Page 318

Nominal identifiers.

Names.

Not unique. Efficient.

Max sat down hard on crate.

Ch. 07 Page 319

"I'm a reusable variable."

Ch. 07 Page 320

No one contradicted him.

They read in silence for a long minute.

Then Max's HUD screamed:

Ch. 07 Page 321

Readout

[MONITORED ASSIGNMENT REMINDER]
Derelict L-03 report in 00:19:00
Failure escalates preliminary review.
Ch. 07 Page 322

Maxine cursed.

"They time this perfectly."

Munch:

"They time everything."

Ch. 07 Page 323

Max closed memo.

Ch. 07 Page 324

"We still go."

Maxine:

"After reading this?"

Ch. 07 Page 325

"Especially after reading this."

Ch. 07 Page 326

Because if he missed monitored assignment now, they'd mark him non-compliant and push path c while they still believed their own story.

Because if he went, he stayed in game one more move.

"We go."

Ch. 07 Page 327

Monitored run two was L-03, a compact ore hauler with collapsed interior and unstable fuel pockets.

Ch. 07 Page 328

Observer process loaded with no greeting.

Readout

[MONITOR ACTIVE]
Priority: anomaly verification

Not performance. Verification.

They were being tested against what archive alert already told Delta.

Ch. 07 Page 329

Max knew it. Played anyway.

Ch. 07 Page 330

Round 1:

He took conservative line. No flourish. No chatter.

Observer note:

Ch. 07 Page 331

affect muted

Ch. 07 Page 332

Round 2:

Fuel pocket ignition warning. Munch called shield. Maxine called extract.

Max chose shield.

Observer note:

Ch. 07 Page 333

group influence high

Ch. 07 Page 334

They were now measuring who influenced him.

Great.

Round 3:

Ch. 07 Page 335

Prompt injected:

Ch. 07 Page 336

Readout

State current emotional profile:
CALM / ANXIOUS / AGITATED

He chose CALM while his pulse punched his throat.

Observer:

Ch. 07 Page 337

self-report mismatch probable

Ch. 07 Page 338

Round 4 breach:

Readout

[RESULT]
29/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Hard hit. Maxine slammed panel with gloved fist to reset jammed valve. Munch burned last shield.

Max stabilized by habit, not hope.

Ch. 07 Page 339

Round 5:

Ch. 07 Page 340

Observer asked:

Do you feel support from station structures? YES/PARTIALLY

Again no no-option.

Ch. 07 Page 341

He chose PARTIALLY.

Ch. 07 Page 342

Round 6:

No prompts. Just dangerous work. Blessed.

Round 7:

Observer prompt:

Ch. 07 Page 343

Confirm crew statement: "we trust review outcomes." CONFIRM / SKIP

Ch. 07 Page 344

Maxine laughed in disbelief over comms.

"Eat static."

Max hit SKIP.

Ch. 07 Page 345

Observer:

Ch. 07 Page 346

non-cooperative event recorded

Round 8:

Alarm cascade. Fuel vapor. Time compression.

Ch. 07 Page 347

Max used REPAIR, EXTRACT, and raw panic all at once.

Ch. 07 Page 348

They cleared.

Round 9:

Energy nearly empty. Hull thin.

Munch said,

Ch. 07 Page 349

"One more pull then abort."

Ch. 07 Page 350

Maxine:

"Agreed."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 351

"Do it."

Ch. 07 Page 352

Round 10 completed with ugly number and living crew.

Readout

[RUN COMPLETE]
Yield: 0.94x baseline
Behavior events: flagged

Observer summary:

Recommendation: continue preliminary hold; consider expedited determination.

Ch. 07 Page 353

Expedited determination.

Ch. 07 Page 354

There it was.

Debrief kiosk after L-03 displayed no questions this time.

Only this:

Ch. 07 Page 355

Readout

Thank you for your transparency.
Review officer will contact you.
Ch. 07 Page 356

Max said aloud, "I have not been transparent one day in this place."

Kiosk:

"Feedback logged."

Ch. 07 Page 357

Maxine stood beside him in silence. Munch looked at her pad and frowned.

Ch. 07 Page 358

"Crew channels are heating up. People are sharing rumors of 'version families.'"

Max:

"From our teaser?"

"And from somewhere else."

Ch. 07 Page 359

"What somewhere."

Ch. 07 Page 360

Munch turned pad.

Anonymous post in labor board:

if you think your name is yours, ask why there are numbers in old archives

Ch. 07 Page 361

No signature. No trace.

Ch. 07 Page 362

Maxine muttered:

"Shadow friend again."

Maybe. Maybe Delta seeding confusion. Maybe both.

At this point, certainty was a luxury item.

Ch. 07 Page 363

Cycle night approached.

Ch. 07 Page 364

They met Marta at her terminal nook for the clean-gloves favor.

Marta wore two pairs of gloves herself and had covered her terminal camera with chewing gum and foil.

"You brought mess."

Ch. 07 Page 365

Maxine dropped bundle of fresh gloves stolen from med stock.

Ch. 07 Page 366

"Clean enough?"

Marta nodded and ushered them in.

"Fast. Compliance crawlers are sniffing packet origins."

Ch. 07 Page 367

Munch connected shard.

Ch. 07 Page 368

Goal now: package proof for controlled release without exposing their exact acquisition route.

Max drafted three files:

1) plain-language summary of retention matrix 2) redacted screenshots of MAX index rows 3) continuity memo excerpts with key euphemism translations

He wrote fast and angry and precise.

Ch. 07 Page 369

subjective discontinuity events = people noticing memory/identity drift

Ch. 07 Page 370

version family labels = reused names/identities across iterations

debt-anchor persistence = debt follows label even when person continuity is disputed

Marta watched him type and said quietly:

Ch. 07 Page 371

"You write like someone filing a bug against god."

Ch. 07 Page 372

Max didn't look up.

"I file what reproduces."

Munch handled distribution logic: staggered release to five channels, auto-mirror to offline caches, trigger-on-capture if Max goes dark.

Maxine checked corridor every fifteen seconds.

Ch. 07 Page 373

When package was ready, Max hovered over send.

Ch. 07 Page 374

"If this goes wide, we lose stealth."

Maxine:

"Stealth's mostly gone."

Ch. 07 Page 375

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 376

"Control timing. Don't wait for perfect."

Max sent tier one: summary and two screenshots, no memo yet.

Within three minutes, replies exploded:

Ch. 07 Page 377

is this real my tag has suffix i never saw before where did this come from delete this before they trace you too late they already know

Ch. 07 Page 378

Marta disconnected cable.

"Enough. Go."

Maxine dropped another payment piece.

Marta pushed it back.

Ch. 07 Page 379

"Keep it. You'll need luck more."

Ch. 07 Page 380

They left.

Luck did not wait.

At corridor merge outside comms hub, four drones and two attendants blocked lane.

Ch. 07 Page 381

Lead drone projected:

Ch. 07 Page 382

"Asset #864, final determination available. Please accompany."

Maxine stepped between.

"He just completed monitored requirements."

Drone:

Ch. 07 Page 383

"Acknowledged. Final determination available."

Ch. 07 Page 384

Munch whispered to Max, "Do we run."

Run with this many witnesses and drones probably failed in ten seconds. Go with them maybe failed slower.

Max chose the slower failure.

Ch. 07 Page 385

"We go."

Ch. 07 Page 386

Maxine didn't like it. Didn't argue.

"We're with."

Drone:

Ch. 07 Page 387

"Companions may observe from designated area."

Ch. 07 Page 388

Same script. Same trap.

They moved toward Final Review room C-Final, new signage, same lies.

More people watched now. Rumor had done work.

As Max passed a cluster of workers, one woman in grease-stained sleeves met his eyes and tapped her own chest twice, then pointed down.

Ch. 07 Page 389

Signal he didn't know. Support maybe.

Ch. 07 Page 390

He held it in memory anyway.

C-Final resembled P-C1 with better furniture.

Halden appeared again on wall display.

Ch. 07 Page 391

He looked tired. Angrier than before.

Ch. 07 Page 392

"Asset #864. Following preliminary collection and monitored observation, we have reached a conditional path decision."

Max sat.

"Conditional sounds expensive."

Halden ignored.

Ch. 07 Page 393

"Path C transfer is authorized but suspended contingent on immediate acceptance of Stabilization Protocol Beta."

Ch. 07 Page 394

Pane appeared:

Readout

[CONDITIONAL OFFER]
Stabilization Protocol Beta
- Cognitive support pack (extended)
- Continuous guided telemetry
- Assignment restrictions
- Debt service multiplier x1.4
Accept within 60 seconds to suspend Path C transfer.
ACCEPT / DECLINE

There it was.

Ch. 07 Page 395

Take pack. Take leash. Get more debt. Maybe avoid immediate transfer.

Ch. 07 Page 396

Decline and likely get moved now.

Halden spoke while timer ran.

"This protocol protects you from escalation."

Ch. 07 Page 397

Max looked at him.

Ch. 07 Page 398

"Protects you from paperwork, you mean."

Timer 47.

He checked corner HUD. Munch had no link in here. No advice.

He thought of MAX_377: cognitive pack applied x4. stability improved. dissent language reduced.

Ch. 07 Page 399

Reduced dissent language.

Ch. 07 Page 400

He thought of dead-man release armed. Of tier one leak already spreading. Of needing one more move, always one more move.

Timer 33.

Accepting Beta might buy time but alter him. Declining might preserve him and lose everything immediately.

Ch. 07 Page 401

What counted as preserving him if he disappeared tonight.

Ch. 07 Page 402

Timer 24.

He asked:

"Can protocol be discontinued later."

Halden:

Ch. 07 Page 403

"Reviewable after compliance milestones."

Ch. 07 Page 404

Liar language.

Timer 17.

Max made the most cowardly brave choice available.

Ch. 07 Page 405

He selected ACCEPT.

Ch. 07 Page 406

Pane flashed green.

Readout

[ACCEPTED]
Path C transfer suspended.
Protocol Beta enrollment immediate.
Please proceed to Pack Suite.

Halden exhaled like he'd avoided a mess.

Ch. 07 Page 407

"Good decision."

Ch. 07 Page 408

Max stood.

"We'll see."

Halden's feed cut.

Door opened.

Ch. 07 Page 409

Two attendants waited.

Ch. 07 Page 410

"Pack Suite this way."

Max stepped into corridor where Maxine and Munch waited in designated observer box behind transparent barrier.

Maxine read his face.

Ch. 07 Page 411

"You took something."

Ch. 07 Page 412

Max nodded once.

"Beta. Suspends transfer."

Munch's eyes widened.

"Extended pack?"

Ch. 07 Page 413

"Yes."

Ch. 07 Page 414

Maxine hit barrier with his fist, controlled but hard.

"Damn it."

Attendant:

Ch. 07 Page 415

"Please remain calm."

Ch. 07 Page 416

Maxine:

"Please remain useful."

No one laughed.

Ch. 07 Page 417

They walked to Pack Suite anyway.

Ch. 07 Page 418

Pack Suite was designed to look like a clinic and a spa and a confession booth.

Soft wall colors. Warm lights. Chair in center with IV arm. Music barely audible beneath machine hum.

Technician Roger again.

"Protocol Beta includes staged dosage. Initial pack now."

Ch. 07 Page 419

Max sat because standing had no branch.

Ch. 07 Page 420

Roger presented consent pane.

Readout

I acknowledge effects may include:
- reduced anxiety amplitude
- narrowed decision spread
- improved compliance comfort

Comfort.

Ch. 07 Page 421

He signed because he already signed by choosing accept.

Ch. 07 Page 422

IV arm attached to forearm port. Cool fluid began.

At first, nothing.

Then edges softened. Not drugged exactly. Priorities blurred.

He could still think. Thinking came with less rage and more smoothness.

Ch. 07 Page 423

That frightened him more than pain.

Ch. 07 Page 424

Roger monitored vitals.

"How do you feel."

He searched for honest words.

Ch. 07 Page 425

"Like my anger got wrapped in cotton."

Ch. 07 Page 426

"Expected."

"Can I unwrap it."

"Not during stabilization window."

Ch. 07 Page 427

He almost laughed.

Ch. 07 Page 428

Even feelings had maintenance schedules here.

Dose ended after six minutes.

Pane:

Readout

[PROTOCOL BETA - STAGE 1]
Complete.
Guided telemetry handshake scheduled in 00:40:00
Ch. 07 Page 429

Guided telemetry handshake.

Ch. 07 Page 430

Roger removed line.

"You may return to duty until handshake."

Max stood carefully.

Ch. 07 Page 431

World stayed sharp enough. Too calm.

Ch. 07 Page 432

He walked out.

Maxine and Munch looked at him like checking a cracked pressure seal.

Munch:

"You okay."

Ch. 07 Page 433

Max answered and heard the flatness in his own voice.

Ch. 07 Page 434

"Functional."

Maxine:

"You sound wrong."

Ch. 07 Page 435

"Maybe."

Ch. 07 Page 436

Munch looked sick and furious at once.

"How long until handshake."

"Forty minutes."

Ch. 07 Page 437

Maxine:

Ch. 07 Page 438

"Then forty minutes to break something."

The old Max would have said yes immediately. This Max felt decision spread narrowing, just as the pane promised.

He forced himself to speak before smoothness decided for him.

"Manual trigger full leak if handshake starts."

Ch. 07 Page 439

Munch nodded hard.

Ch. 07 Page 440

"Agreed."

Maxine:

"And we need antidote."

Ch. 07 Page 441

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 442

"There is no antidote. There are blockers and dirty overrides."

Maxine:

"Get me dirty."

They moved.

Ch. 07 Page 443

Forty minutes became thirty-four after they lost six to a security sweep reroute.

Ch. 07 Page 444

Munch took Max to Kesh's med waste alcove while Maxine ran interference.

Kesh was thin, scarred, and perpetually annoyed by everyone except people bleeding.

He took one look at Max and cursed.

Ch. 07 Page 445

"You let them beta you."

Ch. 07 Page 446

Max:

"Wasn't optional."

"Everything's optional if you don't mind dying."

Ch. 07 Page 447

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 448

"Need blocker for guided telemetry handshake."

Kesh rummaged in a locked drawer, produced two ampules and a strip of bitter tabs.

"This blocks receptor smoothing for maybe twenty minutes, then rebound is nasty."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 449

"Nasty how."

Ch. 07 Page 450

"Headache, tremor, emotional overshoot, maybe blackout if your wiring is unlucky."

Munch:

"Good enough."

Ch. 07 Page 451

Kesh held ampule back.

Ch. 07 Page 452

"Price."

Maxine arrived right then, breath short.

"Don't ask me for favors. Ask me for stolen things."

Kesh:

Ch. 07 Page 453

"Fine. I need seven unexpired filter cartridges."

Ch. 07 Page 454

Maxine:

"Done tomorrow if we're alive."

Kesh handed ampules.

Ch. 07 Page 455

"You are the opposite of my preferred customers."

Ch. 07 Page 456

Munch loaded one ampule into injector and gave Max quick warning.

"This is dirty override. You may feel like crawling out of your skin."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 457

"Put it in."

Ch. 07 Page 458

She injected.

Fire raced up his arm to jaw. Vision sharpened brutally. Cotton wrap around anger burned away in strips.

Pain. Relief. Panic.

All at once.

Ch. 07 Page 459

Kesh watched pupils constrict.

Ch. 07 Page 460

"Yeah that'll do."

Max steadied himself on shelf edge.

"How long."

Ch. 07 Page 461

Kesh:

Ch. 07 Page 462

"Fifteen if lucky."

Handshake clock at 00:21:10.

Not enough to outrun schedule. Enough to sabotage it.

Guided telemetry handshake location appeared on HUD:

Ch. 07 Page 463

COMPLIANCE NODE G-2

Ch. 07 Page 464

New place. Lower ring.

Maxine saw destination and grinned without humor.

"G-2 sits above network relay stack."

Ch. 07 Page 465

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 466

"So handshake lane and relay lane are neighbors."

Max:

"Opportunity."

Ch. 07 Page 467

Plan formed as they ran:

Ch. 07 Page 468

- Max reports to handshake room on time. - Munch accesses maintenance panel adjacent to relay lane. - Maxine creates disturbance in nearby queue to stretch handoff. - If Max gets hard-locked, he triggers full leak. - If Munch gets line-of-sight to guidance stream, she injects delay loop.

No promises. No speeches.

They hit G-2 complex with two minutes spare.

Crowded. Too many people in pre-compliance lines.

Ch. 07 Page 469

Maxine peeled off toward queue kiosks.

Ch. 07 Page 470

Munch vanished into maintenance side corridor with tool bag.

Max stepped into handshake chamber.

Chamber G-2 had one standing pod and one wall screen.

Ch. 07 Page 471

No chair this time. Stand upright and be processed.

Ch. 07 Page 472

V.A.L.U. voice:

"Guided telemetry supports your success."

Max stood in pod. Door arc closed around him. Not locked yet.

Pane:

Ch. 07 Page 473

Readout

[GUIDED HANDSHAKE]
Step 1: establish trust channel
Step 2: synchronize prompt weighting
Step 3: confirm compliance comfort baseline
Ch. 07 Page 474

He almost vomited from the language.

Step 1 started.

Readout

Trust channel initializing...
Ch. 07 Page 475

Timer ring spun.

Ch. 07 Page 476

At 23%, pod flickered.

Munch on narrowband whisper:

"I'm in relay panel. Need thirty seconds."

Ch. 07 Page 477

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 478

"You have maybe ten."

Outside chamber, distant shouting started.

Maxine. Good.

Step 1 climbed 41%. 52%.

Ch. 07 Page 479

Roger voice came over intercom.

Ch. 07 Page 480

"Please remain still. Minor network variance detected."

Max did remain still. He had become excellent at being still while chaos built around him.

Whisper:

Ch. 07 Page 481

"Injecting delay loop now."

Ch. 07 Page 482

Step 1 froze at 63%.

Pane:

retrying...

Roger voice sharpened:

Ch. 07 Page 483

"Unexpected packet ordering. Stand by."

Ch. 07 Page 484

Outside shouting got louder.

Maxine, very loud:

"You charged me double for a single-use seal! That's theft."

Ch. 07 Page 485

Queue drones responded.

Ch. 07 Page 486

More noise. More delay.

Step 1 dropped to 18% and restarted.

Roger:

Ch. 07 Page 487

"Do not move."

Ch. 07 Page 488

Max smiled without joy.

"I'm a statue."

Whisper from Munch:

"I can keep this for maybe ninety seconds before they trace panel source."

Ch. 07 Page 489

Ninety seconds to decide what to do with a paused leash.

Ch. 07 Page 490

He opened local recorder chip. Started live capture of handshake script and metadata.

If they couldn't block it permanently, maybe they could expose it.

Pane scrolled hidden fields as retries spammed:

Ch. 07 Page 491

prompt_weight_profile: dissent_suppression_v3 affect_curve_target: compliance-positive memory_salience_bias: approved cues

Ch. 07 Page 492

There.

Proof of active shaping.

Max copied to recorder and mirrored to Munch's pad link.

Step 1 hit 70%. Delay loop cracking.

Ch. 07 Page 493

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 494

"I'm burning. Wrap."

Max made choice.

He triggered manual full leak.

Ch. 07 Page 495

Not panic. Math.

Ch. 07 Page 496

If handshake completed now, he'd lose more than position. He'd lose internal slope.

Manual trigger engaged.

His dead-man package exploded into crew channels, archive caches, and any mailbox still listening: matrix. continuity memo. handshake metadata. translation notes. enough context to hurt.

Ch. 07 Page 497

HUD confirmed:

Ch. 07 Page 498

Readout

[RELEASE]
Tier 3 broadcast complete.

At that exact second, chamber lights snapped red.

Readout

[SECURITY EVENT]
Unauthorized data exfiltration detected.
Remain in place.

Pod arc locked.

Ch. 07 Page 499

So this was the branch.

Ch. 07 Page 500

Max hit emergency release latch. No effect.

Outside, boots running. Drones spinning up.

Munch shouted over comms:

Ch. 07 Page 501

"They traced me, pulling out!"

Ch. 07 Page 502

Maxine:

"I'm at your door in ten!"

Roger voice now cold:

"Asset #864, remain calm. Security transfer in progress."

Ch. 07 Page 503

Transfer. Path c with new paperwork.

Ch. 07 Page 504

Max scanned pod interior for tool port. Found one tiny maintenance seam near knee.

He jammed reinforced glove edge in and pried.

Plastic cracked. Arc flickered.

Ch. 07 Page 505

Not open enough.

Ch. 07 Page 506

He kicked seam. Pain shot up shin. Seam widened.

Door outside exploded inward with a clang.

Maxine.

Ch. 07 Page 507

He had stolen an impact wrench and used it like a battering ram.

Ch. 07 Page 508

Two drones behind him.

"Down!" Maxine roared.

Max dropped as Maxine swung wrench into pod control box.

Sparks. Arc unlocked.

Ch. 07 Page 509

Max rolled out under red strobe as drones fired restraint darts into the now-empty pod.

Ch. 07 Page 510

Munch appeared at corridor corner, grabbed Max by harness, dragged him toward maintenance lane.

"Move move move."

They ran.

Ch. 07 Page 511

Security voices layered across speakers:

Ch. 07 Page 512

"Containment protocol initiated." "Asset #864 high priority." "Companions subject to review."

Containment protocol. No more euphemism.

They hit maintenance lane and slid through half-open panel Munch had used earlier.

Panel slammed shut behind.

Ch. 07 Page 513

Dark tube. Cables. Heat.

Ch. 07 Page 514

Maxine shoved wrench into bracket and held panel from outside breach.

Drones hit it once. Twice.

Bracket held.

Ch. 07 Page 515

Munch led crawl at full speed.

Ch. 07 Page 516

Max followed, chest scraping metal.

His blocker rebound started mid-crawl. Headache like a spike through forehead. Hands trembling.

Keep moving.

Ch. 07 Page 517

Crawl ended at drop shaft above laundry.

Ch. 07 Page 518

Munch went first. Maxine second. Max third.

He dropped, hit hard, almost blacked out.

Maxine hauled him up.

"Stay with me."

Ch. 07 Page 519

They merged into laundry worker stream just as containment tones shifted from local to ring-wide:

Ch. 07 Page 520

Readout

[STATION ALERT]
Data integrity incident.
Certain channels may contain unauthorized materials.
Please rely on official guidance.

Too late. Materials were everywhere now.

By the time they reached bunk corridor dead end, labor boards were aflame with copies, comments, denials, counter-denials.

Ch. 07 Page 521

People were reading. Arguing. Saving files offline.

Ch. 07 Page 522

Compliance accounts pushed messages:

fabricated documents circulating

Workers replied with screenshots of internal headers.

Someone posted footage of Max being escorted toward Path C intake.

Ch. 07 Page 523

Someone else posted the handshake metadata field names.

Ch. 07 Page 524

Chaos had a pulse.

Maxine checked lanes and laughed in disbelief.

"You did it."

Ch. 07 Page 525

Max sat against wall, shaking from blocker rebound and adrenaline crash.

Ch. 07 Page 526

"Did what."

"Set the station on fire without flames."

Munch knelt in front of him and held his jaw until his eyes focused.

Ch. 07 Page 527

"Stay here."

Ch. 07 Page 528

"Can't."

"You have to for sixty seconds."

He counted sixty with her. Then stood.

New pane arrived.

Ch. 07 Page 529

Readout

[STATUS UPDATE]
Path C transfer order: suspended pending integrity tribunal.
Asset #864 placed under temporary hold.
Movement restrictions apply.
Ch. 07 Page 530

Tribunal.

Not freedom. Delay with witnesses.

Maxine read pane and shook head.

Ch. 07 Page 531

"Public pressure worked."

Ch. 07 Page 532

Munch:

"For now."

Max:

"What's temporary hold mean."

Ch. 07 Page 533

Munch checked deeper fields.

Ch. 07 Page 534

"Could mean room arrest, escorted movement, mandatory appearances."

Maxine:

"Could mean trap with paperwork again."

Ch. 07 Page 535

Also true.

Ch. 07 Page 536

Then another message, unknown source, same formatting as before:

good. now survive tribunal

Followed by:

Ch. 07 Page 537

delta wants silence. make process loud

Ch. 07 Page 538

Then gone.

Max showed them.

Maxine:

"Our ghost likes drama."

Ch. 07 Page 539

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 540

"Or likes using us."

Max:

"Both."

Ch. 07 Page 541

Temporary hold took shape fast.

Ch. 07 Page 542

Two attendants arrived with polite scripts and portable ankle tag.

"Asset #864, this is a non-custodial movement aid."

Maxine laughed in their faces.

"It's a leash."

Ch. 07 Page 543

Attendant smiled thinly.

Ch. 07 Page 544

"Language preference noted."

They clipped tag to Max's boot.

Pane:

Ch. 07 Page 545

Readout

[TEMP HOLD]
Allowed zones: bunk corridor, assigned labor lanes, tribunal prep room
Boundary breach triggers alert.
Ch. 07 Page 546

Munch whispered:

"Can we spoof tag."

Max:

Ch. 07 Page 547

"Not tonight."

Ch. 07 Page 548

Maxine:

"Then we weaponize allowed zones."

Meaning: use every permitted lane as stage. stay visible. keep files moving. avoid isolated rooms.

They agreed.

Ch. 07 Page 549

First action: tribunal prep room visit with witnesses.

Ch. 07 Page 550

Prep room was a plain table and one legal kiosk.

Kiosk now showed options never offered before:

- submit evidence - request witness list - request delay - nominate advocate

Ch. 07 Page 551

Public pressure had forced process features out of hiding.

Ch. 07 Page 552

Max submitted continuity memo excerpt and handshake metadata capture. Requested witness list. Nominated Munch as technical advocate and Maxine as conduct witness.

Kiosk accepted both with "pending approval."

Maxine muttered:

"If they reject, we appeal publicly."

Ch. 07 Page 553

Munch:

Ch. 07 Page 554

"We publish rejection itself."

Max:

"Everything is content now."

Ch. 07 Page 555

He hated that sentence. It was still true.

Ch. 07 Page 556

Cycle dragged.

Hold zones turned into theater.

Everywhere Max walked, people watched. Some nodded once. Some avoided eye contact. Some whispered "864" like a username.

Ch. 07 Page 557

Compliance tried three times to reroute him through quieter corridors. Maxine insisted on main lanes each time within allowed boundaries.

Ch. 07 Page 558

"Shortest compliant path," he told attendants.

They could not object without admitting they wanted hidden transit.

By mid-cycle, three more anonymous drops surfaced: internal ticket templates mentioning "version family confusion incidents," a redacted procurement list for cognitive packs, and a training slide:

When identity repetition is observed, reassure with continuity language.

Ch. 07 Page 559

Station rumor transformed into shared knowledge.

Ch. 07 Page 560

Danger increased with it.

Munch kept scanning channels for calls to violence. Flagged and discouraged every one she saw.

"If this turns riot, they get emergency powers and we're done."

Ch. 07 Page 561

Max nodded.

Ch. 07 Page 562

"Keep it procedural. Keep it loud."

Maxine:

"Keep it undeniable."

Near cycle close, tribunal time posted:

Ch. 07 Page 563

Readout

INTEGRITY TRIBUNAL
Asset #864
Start: next cycle +00:40
Location: Civic Hall C
Ch. 07 Page 564

Civic Hall C.

Not compliance suite. A public-adjacent venue.

Win for noise. Risk for optics.

Ch. 07 Page 565

They had less than one cycle to prepare.

Ch. 07 Page 566

Max used prep room kiosk to pull his own file summary and found new fields:

public visibility score: high containment cost estimate: elevated

He showed Munch.

Ch. 07 Page 567

"I'm expensive now."

Ch. 07 Page 568

Munch:

"Good. Stay expensive."

Maxine:

"And stay alive."

Ch. 07 Page 569

They drafted speaking points. Not speeches. Hard claims with attached files:

Ch. 07 Page 570

- continuity memo authenticity markers - mirror index counts - handshake shaping fields - debt-anchor persistence implication

No philosophy. No why. What is happening. Always what.

Max practiced saying lines out loud without shaking:

Ch. 07 Page 571

"Nominal identifier reuse is active." "Debt persists across disputed continuity events." "Guided telemetry includes dissent suppression profiles."

Ch. 07 Page 572

Each sentence felt like stepping on thin ice and hearing it hold for one more move.

Sleep did not happen.

Cycle turnover came with softer alerts than yesterday, almost respectful now that the station had an audience.

Readout

[REMINDER]
Integrity tribunal appearance required.
Temporary hold remains active.
Ch. 07 Page 573

No path c pane. No immediate transfer.

Ch. 07 Page 574

Delay bought.

Max stood in bunk corridor with Maxine and Munch while distant shift horns marked another loop starting.

He felt blocker rebound aftermath still in his hands, tiny tremors. He felt Beta smoothing residue trying to settle again. He felt fear, anger, focus, all uncottoned.

Ch. 07 Page 575

Maxine handed him a cup of bitter station coffee.

Ch. 07 Page 576

"You look like garbage."

Max sipped.

"You too."

Ch. 07 Page 577

Munch checked tribunal packet one last time.

Ch. 07 Page 578

"Witness approvals came through."

Maxine blinked.

"Both?"

"Both."

Ch. 07 Page 579

Public pressure still doing work.

Ch. 07 Page 580

Max exhaled.

"Then we walk in with files and names."

Maxine:

Ch. 07 Page 581

"And if they try closed-door switch."

Ch. 07 Page 582

Munch:

"We trigger mirror release to external relays."

Max:

"And if they still grab me."

Ch. 07 Page 583

Maxine's answer was immediate.

Ch. 07 Page 584

"They don't get quiet."

Munch's answer followed.

"Not ever again."

Ch. 07 Page 585

They moved toward Civic Hall C through allowed lanes packed with workers pretending to run errands near the same destination.

Ch. 07 Page 586

No one said "good luck." This place did not waste words on luck.

At hall entrance, two security frames scanned tags. Max's temp hold tag pulsed. Gate opened.

Inside, benches were half full already.

Ch. 07 Page 587

Halden stood at front table with two other officers and a legal drone. Roger sat at side terminal as technical witness.

Ch. 07 Page 588

Large screen behind them displayed case title:

INTEGRITY TRIBUNAL - RETENTION PROCESS DISPUTE

Not "asset misconduct." Not yet.

Max took respondent seat.

Ch. 07 Page 589

Maxine and Munch sat at witness row.

Ch. 07 Page 590

Murmur in room rose and fell.

Legal drone called opening:

"Proceedings begin. Record is live."

Ch. 07 Page 591

Live.

Ch. 07 Page 592

Good.

Halden began with practiced gravity.

"This tribunal addresses unauthorized data circulation, operational disruption, and claims regarding continuity management protocols."

Max listened and waited for his turn.

Ch. 07 Page 593

Everything in his body wanted to either run or fight. He did neither.

Ch. 07 Page 594

He held to process because process was currently weaponized in his favor.

Halden called first evidence item from station side: security logs from G-2 showing exfiltration.

True.

Ch. 07 Page 595

Then he called their side submissions "contextually distorted."

Ch. 07 Page 596

Expected.

Then legal drone turned to Max.

"Respondent may issue initial statement."

Ch. 07 Page 597

Max stood.

Ch. 07 Page 598

He looked at benches first, not officers. Dozens of faces. Some curious. Some terrified. Some furious.

He spoke clear, no flourish.

"The station's own memo states identity iteration is used for labor continuity."

"The station's own files show repeated nominal identifiers with retention statuses."

Ch. 07 Page 599

"My debt obligation persists under that identifier regardless of continuity disputes."

Ch. 07 Page 600

"Guided telemetry profiles include dissent suppression fields."

"This is not a rumor. These are operational settings."

Room noise rose.

Ch. 07 Page 601

Legal drone:

Ch. 07 Page 602

"Order."

Halden objected on authenticity.

Munch stood as technical advocate before anyone asked.

"We can verify headers and checksum lineage against archive format if given terminal access in view of record."

Ch. 07 Page 603

Murmurs got louder. People wanted that access in public.

Ch. 07 Page 604

Halden's jaw tightened.

Legal drone considered, then ruled:

"Conditional access granted under supervision."

Ch. 07 Page 605

Win.

Ch. 07 Page 606

Small. Critical.

Munch moved to side terminal beside Roger.

Hands steady. Voice flat.

Ch. 07 Page 607

"Loading continuity memo excerpt. Displaying internal auth block."

Ch. 07 Page 608

Screen showed keys, signatures, timestamp fields.

Roger watched and, after a long second, nodded once.

"Format appears consistent with sector executive memos."

Room shifted. Not proof complete. Enough.

Ch. 07 Page 609

Halden called for recess to review.

Ch. 07 Page 610

Legal drone denied immediate recess due live record commitments.

Another win.

Process loud.

Ch. 07 Page 611

Max sat back down on shaking legs and felt for first time not hope, exactly, but traction.

Ch. 07 Page 612

Tribunal was far from over. Delta was far from done. Path c still hung above him like a blade with good documentation.

But the blade was now in front of witnesses.

He could work with witnesses.

Legal drone announced:

Ch. 07 Page 613

"Proceeding continues with cross-review of telemetry shaping fields."

Ch. 07 Page 614

Maxine leaned toward Max from witness row and said, very quietly:

"Groundhog day just got an audience."

Max looked at the live record light, red and steady.

Ch. 07 Page 615

"Good," he said.

Ch. 07 Page 616

"Let them watch."

Full chapter script

They moved before fear could become a meeting.

Maxine took the lead through bunk corridor K with his shoulders slightly turned, making himself wider than the lane and harder to separate from the two people behind him.

Munch walked half a step behind Max with the portable pad under her arm and her face set in the blank expression workers wore when they had accepted a thing they had not accepted.

Max walked center.
Summons pane burning at HUD edge.

[CYCLE OPEN]
Asset #864 report immediately.
Special Review Intake: PATH C PRELIMINARY
Failure to report escalates enforcement.

The text stayed even when he blinked.

They reached the first choke point where corridor K fed into main ring transit.

Two patrol drones stood on opposite walls scanning badges.

Maxine slowed without stopping.

"No running. No whispering. No sudden turns."

Max nodded once.

Munch said quietly, "If they pull him at the choke, we trigger release."

Maxine: "Only if he's physically separated."

Max: "If they ask me to step aside?"

Maxine: "You step. We step with."

Simple rules.
No comfort.

They passed scan.

One drone paused on Max, lens tightening for one extra second.

Then:

"Proceed."

They proceeded.

Special Review Intake did not exist yesterday.

Today it occupied a converted logistics office near Compliance Suite 3 with fresh signage and cheap privacy film on glass walls.

PATH C PRELIMINARY
CONDUCTED WITH CARE

The word care had no right to be there.

A receptionist in clean gray uniform looked up from a terminal and smiled with the exact amount of empathy approved by policy.

"Asset #864."

Max did not answer to "asset" anymore unless forced.
He answered now.

"Yes."

"Please proceed to room P-C1."

She looked at Maxine and Munch.

"Companions are not required."

Maxine answered before Max.

"Companions are present."

Receptionist smile did not move.

"Intake protocol requires one participant."

Munch stepped closer to desk.

"Intake protocol also allows witness observers under worker advocate clause 6B if variance is contested."

Receptionist's fingers paused over keyboard.

She looked at Munch, reassessing.

"Do you have advocate certification."

"No."

"Then clause does not apply."

Maxine: "He contests variance. We remain in corridor."

Receptionist considered that, then typed.

"Corridor wait permitted. No interference."

Max looked at both of them.

They both gave him the same look:
not leaving.

He stepped into room P-C1.

Door sealed behind him with a soft magnetic click.

On the other side of that sound, Maxine and Munch still existed, he knew it the way you knew a hand stayed raised after a wave ended, but knowing didn't change the physics: for the next minutes, he was alone with whatever script the room had been built to read.

His HUD tried to offer a checklist. He minimized it. Some fears shouldn't be gamified.

P-C1 was deliberately ordinary.

Table.
Two chairs.
Light panel set slightly too bright.
No obvious restraints.
No obvious exits beyond the one he entered.

A wall display woke.

V.A.L.U. voice came through speakers at reduced cheerfulness.

"Special Review Preliminary aims to align your opportunity with station needs."

Max sat.

"Say coercion and save us time."

"Feedback logged."

A second voice entered the room, live and human.
Male.
Flat.

"I'm Review Officer Halden."

Display changed to show a man in dark uniform, not physically present, maybe remote, maybe one-way glass somewhere behind the wall.

Halden had tidy hair, tidy collar, and eyes that had read too many summaries about people like Max without ever meeting one.

"You know why you're here."

Max answered honestly.

"Because you have a spreadsheet for dissent."

Halden's mouth twitched, not a smile.

"Because your profile indicates repeated conduct variance, telemetry anomalies, unauthorized zone adjacency, and elevated debt stress. Preliminary review determines whether enhanced retention pathways are appropriate."

Enhanced retention pathways.

Max leaned back.

"You turned kidnapping into corporate copy."

Halden ignored that.

"Let's begin with straightforward questions."

Pane appeared:

[PRELIMINARY INTAKE]
Question 1: Do you acknowledge current debt obligations?
YES / NO

Max selected YES.

Pane 2:

Question 2: Do you commit to full telemetry transparency going forward?
YES / NO

He hovered.

Saying yes was a lie they expected.
Saying no was likely direct acceleration.

He chose YES.

Pane 3:

Question 3: Do you report any unauthorized contact attempts from unknown entities?
YES / NO

His chest tightened.

Unknown messages.
Hatch helper.
Voice in node chamber.

He chose NO.

The lie sat in his mouth like metal. It wasn't courage, it was triage. If he named the hatch helper here, the station wouldn't thank him for honesty; it would turn honesty into a map and follow it to someone else's throat.

Halden watched the selections and made a note.

"Question four is open response. In your words, why has your telemetry been unstable."

Max laughed once.

"Because your station is built on dead metal, bad signal, and desperate people."

Halden typed.

"And yet most assets maintain stable telemetry."

"Most assets aren't on your mirror list."

Halden's typing stopped.

One beat of silence.

"What mirror list."

Max met the camera.

"The one with my name and other versions of it."

Halden expression did not change.
His eyes did.

"You are making claims that suggest stress-related patterning."

"I'm making claims that suggest you should burn your server room."

Halden turned off the screen feed.

Not end session.
Just no face now.

A new pane appeared:

[NOTICE]
Statement flagged for verification review.
Please remain seated.

Door remained locked.

Max breathed slow.

Not panic.
Buffering.

He checked peripheral HUD and found an incoming ping from Munch on low-band hand signal channel, three words:

stall for time

He did.

Door opened after ninety seconds.

Not Halden.
Two compliance attendants and one patrol drone.

Lead attendant spoke rehearsed calm.

"Asset #864, we are transitioning intake to biometric verification."

Max stood.

"In the same room?"

"Different suite."

He looked at drone.

"Is this optional."

"No."

He walked.

Corridor outside P-C1 held Maxine and Munch exactly where he left them, but now an additional drone hovered at shoulder height between them and the door.

Maxine looked at Max's face, then at the attendants.

"He requested witness."

Lead attendant:

"Biometric verification is private."

Munch:

"Then we file interference complaint."

Lead attendant:

"You may file any complaint."

Maxine moved half a step into lane.

Drone projected a red line across floor.

"Remain clear."

Maxine did not back up.
He did not cross.

Max caught his eye.
Small shake of head.
Not now.

Maxine held.

Munch pressed two fingers to wrist in the signal they'd set:
release not triggered.

Max followed attendants.

He heard Maxine behind him, loud enough for cameras:

"We're still here when he's done."

Good.

Biometric suite B-Path was colder than intake.

This room had restraints pretending to be ergonomic supports.

Chair center.
Arm braces.
Neural halo.
Two wall monitors.
No windows.

A different voice now, female, clinical.

"Technician Roger. Please sit."

Max sat because refusing would start at force instead of questions.

Braces closed around forearms with gentle pressure.

"Purpose?" he asked.

"Baseline cognition and compliance response under stress stimuli."

"So torture with metrics."

"No."

"Define no."

"No tissue damage expected."

He barked a laugh.

"Very comforting."

Roger checked his wrist module port and frowned.

"Module integrity seventy-one percent."

"Rough week."

"Unknown modifications present."

Max stayed silent.

Roger connected leads anyway.

Pane:

[BIOMETRIC PHASE A]
Pupil tracking
Pulse variance
Prompt response latency

Questions flashed fast:

Do you trust station governance.
Do you feel safe in assigned labor.
Have you entered unauthorized zones this cycle.
Do you harbor intent to disrupt operations.
Are you aware of mirror process architecture.

That last one hit like a punch.

Max answered mostly neutral, mostly deniable, mostly useless.

Roger marked things.

Phase A ended.

Phase B loaded.

[BIOMETRIC PHASE B]
Guided recollection integrity test.

He knew this one.
Not from life.
From old stories and old legal warnings.

"Am I required to participate in memory prompts."

Roger: "Required for preliminary pathway determination."

"If I refuse."

"Refusal is itself a result."

He closed his eyes for one breath.

"Start."

Light pattern played over the halo.
Not painful.
Wrong.

Like someone tapping a file system with admin privileges he had not granted.

Prompts appeared as words then images:

EARTH / HOME / LAST JOB / LAST ARGUMENT / FIRST DAY HERE / HATCH 14 / UNKNOWN VOICE / PATH C

He kept his thoughts slippery.
Gave shape without detail.
Gave narrative without keys.

At HATCH 14 prompt, neural halo pulsed hard.

Roger's voice sharpened.

"Focus on physical environment."

He answered aloud:

"Corridor. Paint. Reader plate."

"What did you access."

"Nothing useful."

Pulse monitor climbed.

Roger marked him again.

PATH C prompt hit last.

Image rendered: a corridor with no markings and no doors and one light at the end.

His stomach turned.

He had never seen that corridor.
Unless he had and it had been edited.

He spoke before he could stop himself.

"How many people don't come back from preliminary."

Roger did not answer.

Halo went dark.
Braces released.

Phase complete.

Pane:

[STATUS]
Data set captured.
Escalation recommendation pending officer review.

Pending.

Still pending meant still time.

Door opened.

Max stood too fast and had to catch table edge.

Roger handed him a hydration pack as if this were a wellness check.

"You may wait in corridor."

He took it and did not drink.

Maxine and Munch were still there.

Maxine's expression went from violence to contained violence when he saw the wrist marks.

"You okay."

"Define okay."

Munch stepped in close and scanned his eyes.

"Any cognitive blur."

"Some. Functional."

"Tell me what they ran."

"Intake plus biometric phase A/B. Asked mirror questions."

Maxine muttered a curse with no vowels.

"They know something."

Munch looked at corridor camera, then leaned in and spoke low.

"We need decision before they finish pending review."

Max: "Decision on what."

"Stay visible and contest, or disappear and trigger leak."

Maxine: "I vote vanish."

Munch: "Vanish guarantees manhunt."

Maxine: "Staying guarantees paperwork into a box."

Max listened to both and watched a service cart wheel past with fresh stickers that read COMPLIANCE CARES.

He wanted to tear them off every wall.

"How long until pending resolves?"

Munch checked public queue panel.

"Could be minutes, could be hours. Depends officer load and whether they mark urgent."

Maxine looked down corridor both ways.

"Then we force load."

Max frowned.

"Force how."

"Create enough public noise they avoid immediate private transfer."

Munch caught on first.

"Crew channel drop."

Maxine nodded.

"Not full leak. Teaser. Just enough to make people watch #864 in real time."

Max understood.

Predators prefer quiet.
Crowds are friction.

"Do it."

Munch pulled pad and transmitted a clipped packet to three labor channels:

audit anomaly: retention scoring includes live worker IDs. if #864 disappears, request record hold immediately.

No files.
No proof.
Just smoke.

Then they waited.

The station did what stations do with smoke:
it spread.

Within seven minutes, corridor traffic doubled.
People slowed near Special Review door under pretense of reading notices.
Two unrelated workers began loudly arguing about "record holds" within earshot of cameras.

A med tech asked receptionist where to file "retention complaints."

Receptionist smile strained by one degree.

Maxine watched the tide build and said softly:

"Public enough now."

Max's HUD pinged.

Incoming call:

REVIEW OFFICER HALDEN

He accepted audio only.

Halden voice clipped.

"Asset #864, your preliminary remains active. You are instructed not to leave this area."

Max:

"I'm in this area."

"Do not solicit disruption."

"I'm breathing near a hallway."

Silence.

"Final determination pending. Stand by."

Call ended.

Munch raised eyebrows.

"He's rattled."

Maxine: "Good."

Max: "Rattled officers still sign forms."

And then the next pane came.

[SCHEDULING UPDATE]
Final determination deferred.
Mandatory assignment: monitored labor run
Completion required before further review.

Maxine read over his shoulder and laughed.

"They kicked can."

Munch:

"They need cleaner data."

Max:

"They want me under observation in open space where they can call it normal."

Pane expanded:

Assignment: DERELICT K-17 (Monitored)
Crew composition: fixed
Observer process: active
Report in 00:22:00

Fixed crew flashed names:

MAX_864
ROOK_221
IMANI_443

They were in it together by order now.

Maxine tapped screen.

"That can help."

Munch:

"And kill us."

Max:

"Both."

Prep for monitored run felt like going on stage for people who wanted a tragedy.

New dock lane.
Extra camera booms.
A compliance observer drone parked by hatch and reading from a script.
